death_threat.title: ⚠️ Death threat detected
death_threat.legal: In South Korea, death threats are punishable under Article 283 of the Criminal Code (up to 3 years in prison). Keep evidence: screenshots, URL, timestamp, username. File a report online at the Cyber Bureau of the Korean National Police Agency (https://ecrm.police.go.kr). Report the threat to the platform (YouTube, Twitter, etc.) using their harassment/death threat forms.
death_threat.psychological: Do not leave the victim alone. Immediately inform their manager and security personnel. Speak gently: 'We received a worrying message, but we are taking all measures to protect you. You are not alone.' Avoid showing them the screen. Offer professional psychological support (helpline 1393).
actions: Do not leave the victim alone today

doxxing.title: ⚠️ Personal information leak (doxxing)
doxxing.legal: Disclosing personal information without consent is illegal in South Korea (Act on Promotion of Information and Communications Network Utilization and Information Protection). File a complaint with the cybercrime bureau. Request the platform to remove the information immediately.
doxxing.psychological: Change your passwords. Enable two-factor authentication. Do not share your location. Contact a trusted person for support. Helpline 1393 for cyber harassment.

school_bullying.title: ⚠️ School bullying detected
school_bullying.legal: South Korea's Act on the Prevention of School Violence provides sanctions. Report to the school or the local Office of Education. The school must investigate within 10 days.
school_bullying.psychological: Talk to a teacher or school counselor. You are not alone. Helpline 117 for youth protection. Do not respond to bullies; keep evidence.

sexual_harassment.title: ⚠️ Sexual harassment detected
sexual_harassment.legal: Online sexual harassment is punishable by law (fines or imprisonment). Preserve evidence. File a complaint with the cybercrime bureau. Contact the Korean Women's Hotline 1366.
sexual_harassment.psychological: Do not blame yourself. Seek support from a trusted person. Contact the Sexual Violence Counseling Center (1366). Avoid engaging with the harasser.

hate_speech.title: ⚠️ Hate speech detected
hate_speech.legal: Hate speech can be reported to the Korean National Human Rights Commission. Platform terms of service prohibit hate speech; report the content.
hate_speech.psychological: Do not engage with haters. Block and report. Surround yourself with supportive people. Take a break from social media if needed.

default.title: ⚠️ Toxic content detected
default.legal: Preserve evidence (screenshots, URLs). Report the content to the platform. If you feel in danger, contact the police (112).
default.psychological: Take care of yourself. Talk to someone you trust. Helpline 1393 is available for cyber harassment.
